scenes.line: Some things matter too much to forget. So we built a place that remembers — a public record for the world's most contested ideas. Kept by many. Owned by no one.

It works like science, out in the open. Anyone can file a claim, with its source attached. Real people review it — free to agree, or to disagree. And it only stays while it holds up. Every step, handed back to the public.

The archive begins as a blank page. Together, peers add the big subjects worth keeping a record of — like Non-Human Intelligence, or Psychic Abilities. Each one grows new topics over time. And when something no longer holds up, the community can gently let it go. The map keeps pace with what we actually know.

Not all evidence is equal — and that's okay. Everything is filed in three honest tiers. Tier one: peer-reviewed and declassified. Tier two: documented sources. Tier three: first-hand testimony. You always know exactly what you're looking at.

Every entry sets off on a little journey. It joins the queue, opens for review, and once enough peers agree — it becomes part of the record. If it doesn't, it simply steps aside, and can always try again. And even settled records stay open. Anything can be revisited. The story is allowed to change.
Here's the part we love. Every vote is signed, straight from a peer's own wallet — so no one can ever vote in your name. And every vote carries a note: the reasoning, saved right beside the decision.

But what is that wallet? You may know MetaMask — the little fox. Think of it as your signature for the web: no sign-up, no password, no email. It simply proves you are you. You stay in control, and we never hold the keys.

Being in the record doesn't mean it's proven true. It means that, right now, the people who study this stand behind it. Evidence isn't proof. The question stays open — and honestly, that's the whole point.

And no one sits at the top deciding who counts. A peer is invited by another peer, welcomed by the group, and verified automatically. If someone needs to step away, that's handled the same way. The community looks after itself.

Projects like this usually fail in three ways. The host gets pressured. The moderators get bought. The funding dries up. So we designed each one out. The blockchain is the host. Signatures are the moderators. And everyone covers their own small costs.
No likes. No endless feed. No ads, no tracking, nothing for sale. No anonymous moderators. No off-switch. No owner. All of it — left out, on purpose.
A monument freezes one version of the truth, forever. A map keeps growing, and expects to be corrected. We're not building a monument. We're drawing a map — together.
So, come on in. Read it. Add to it. Earn your place. And help keep it honest. The Disclosure Platform is open — and there's a seat for you.
Kept by many. Owned by no one.
